verses.text: Elihu calls the wise to weigh his words and 'choose to us judgment.' He appeals to careful discernment. Truth is found not by feeling but by sober, shared examination under God.
Elihu calls the wise to weigh his words and 'choose to us judgment.' He appeals to careful discernment. Truth is found not by feeling but by sober, shared examination under God.

He quotes Job as claiming, 'I am righteous, and God hath taken away my judgment,' as if it profits nothing to please God. Elihu thinks this dangerous talk. In pain, even the godly may say things that wrong the justice of God.

Far be it from God to do wickedness, Elihu insists; He renders to each according to his work. God cannot be unjust. This is bedrock truth: the Judge of all the earth will always do right.

Who gave God charge over the earth, and if He withdrew His breath, all flesh would perish together? He owes His power to none. The God who upholds every life is answerable to no one above Him.

Would one who hates justice govern the world? God shows no partiality to princes over the poor, for all are His handiwork. His justice is perfectly impartial. Rank and riches carry no weight before the Maker of all.

God's eyes are on every step a man takes; no darkness can hide the wicked, and He breaks the mighty without inquiry. Nothing escapes His sight. The all-seeing God needs no trial to know and judge the truth.

He overturns the wicked in the night and strikes them openly because they turned from Him and crushed the poor. Their fall is His just sentence. Oppression of the helpless cries to heaven and is surely answered.

When God gives quiet, none can trouble; when He hides His face, none can find Him — over nations and individuals alike. His will governs all. Our peace and our trouble are alike in His sovereign hand.

When God gives quiet, none can trouble; when He hides His face, none can find Him — over nations and individuals alike. His will governs all. Our peace and our trouble are alike in His sovereign hand.

Should God rule by Job's preferences? He will recompense as He sees fit, whether men accept it or not. God's judgments are not subject to our approval. To submit to His wisdom is the part of humble faith.

Men of understanding, Elihu says, will judge that Job has spoken without knowledge and added rebellion to his sin. He presses the charge hard. Yet even here he calls Job to humble himself rather than defend his words.
